layman_headline: Crypto market is gripped by extreme fear — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 22/100, with Bitcoin sliding below $62,500 amid geopolitical tensions with Iran and an upcoming U.S. inflation report. Altcoins are getting hit even harder, with most swing candidates down 10-33% over the past week. While fear often creates buying opportunities, none of the candidates today have a convincing technical setup — charts are mostly bearish, risk/reward ratios are poor, and Bitcoin dominance at 56% means money is flowing out of altcoins into Bitcoin.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't found a bottom yet — better entries will appear once the CPI data drops and geopolitical uncertainty settles. Don't try to catch a falling knife.
expert_reasoning: The macro backdrop is hostile for altcoin entries: BTC dominance at 56% signals capital rotation away from alts, the Iran conflict is reigniting risk-off behavior, and a CPI report is imminent which could trigger further volatility. Among candidates, GRASS (-33% 7d) looks like a capitulation play but has bearish chart structure and poor R/R (0.69x). BONK has deeply bearish charts (-1.40 bias) with a bearish marubozu — classic avoid signal. MON has a bullish marubozu candle but is in a downtrend below all MAs. JTO is the strongest candidate with an uptrend, moderately bullish MA structure, and 1.05x R/R, but RSI at 59 is elevated and the $288M government BTC/ETH transfer to Coinbase creates overhang risk across the market. The prudent play is to wait for either JTO to pull back to $0.60 support or for the CPI catalyst to resolve before deploying capital.
risk_factors: Iran conflict escalation could trigger a broader risk-off selloff pushing BTC below $60K and dragging all alts further down
U.S. CPI report could come in hot, fueling July Fed rate hike bets and crushing crypto prices
U.S. government moving $288M in seized crypto to Coinbase suggests potential selling pressure on BTC and ETH
watch_list: U.S. CPI inflation data release — hot print could crash markets, cool print could spark relief rally
JTO price action around $0.60 support — a bounce there with volume would be an actionable entry
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from 23.9 weekly avg; a move above 30 would signal sentiment stabilization
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with Bitcoin at $62,450, pressured by geopolitical tensions and pre-CPI anxiety. High BTC dominance at 56% makes this a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Extreme Fear with macro catalysts pending — capital preservation trumps opportunity hunting until the CPI dust settles.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend suggests we're near a bottom, but not yet confirmed — contrarian signal to watch but not act on prematurely.
Bitcoin holding $62,600 amid Iran conflict and looming CPI report creates binary event risk that makes new entries inadvisable before resolution.
U.S. government moved $288M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime, creating potential sell-side overhang across the entire market.
No candidate has RSI below 30 (truly oversold) — BONK at 33 is closest but its bearish marubozu and -1.40 chart bias make it a falling knife rather than a bounce candidate.
JTO is the only candidate with positive chart bias (+0.40) and bullish MA structure, but doji indecision and 59 RSI suggest waiting for a better entry near $0.60 support.
